draft.introduction: Psalm 100 is a brief hymn of praise calling all the earth to worship with joy and thanksgiving. Its five verses encapsulate the theology of Israelite worship: God made us, we are his, and his faithfulness endures forever.

translation.text: Make a joyful noise to the Lord, all the earth! Serve the Lord with gladness! Come into his presence with singing! Know that the Lord, he is God! It is he who made us, and we are his; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture. For the Lord is good; his steadfast love endures forever, and his faithfulness to all generations.
translation.original_text: ἀλαλάξατε τῷ Κυρίῳ πᾶσα ἡ γῆ

translator_notes.note: There is a textual variant between “he made us and we are his” (reading “lo” as “to him”) and “he made us and not we ourselves” (reading “lo” as “not”). The Hebrew consonants are identical; only the vowel pointing differs. Both readings are theologically significant.
